# Storage and Backup Services

## LRZ Online Storage (NAS)

### Overview

The Leibniz Supercomputing Centre (LRZ) provides central Online Storage (NAS) -- a secure, highly available storage system for all TUM members. Every TUM member automatically receives personal storage space. Additionally, organizational units can request project storage for collaborative work.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Training Video
An 18-minute instructional video "Daten sicher speichern, teilen und synchronisieren" (Securely store, share, and synchronize data) is available through TUM IT resources for an overview of storage services.
:::

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "NAS Service Architecture"
flowchart TB
    subgraph Clients["Client Access"]
        Win["Windows<br/>(SMB)"]
        Linux["Linux<br/>(NFS)"]
        Mac["macOS<br/>(SMB/NFS)"]
        Browser["Browser<br/>(WebDisk)"]
    end

    subgraph Access["Access Methods"]
        LAN["MWN Network"]
        VPN["VPN Connection"]
        WebDAV["WebDAV"]
    end

    subgraph NAS["LRZ NAS Infrastructure"]
        FS["Fileserver"]
        LDAP["TUM LDAP Authentication"]
        Backup["Backup Services"]
    end

    Win --> LAN
    Linux --> LAN
    Mac --> LAN
    Browser --> LAN

    Win --> VPN
    Linux --> VPN
    Mac --> VPN

    LAN --> FS
    VPN --> FS
    WebDAV --> FS

    FS --> LDAP
    FS --> Backup
```

### Storage Types

| Type | Description | Provisioning |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| **Personal Storage** | Secure, highly available storage for every TUM member | Automatic with TUM account |
| **Project Storage (Projektlaufwerke)** | Shared storage for organizational units and teams | Request through IT Service Center |

### Directory Types

The NAS provides several types of directories:

| Directory Type | Description |
|----------------|-------------|
| **Personal Directory** | Private storage for individual users |
| **Project Drives (Projektlaufwerke)** | Shared storage for teams and projects |
| **Temporary Directories** | Short-term storage (may have retention limits) |
| **Public Directories** | Accessible to group members |
| **www-public Directories** | Content accessible via web for file downloads |

### Access Methods

| Method | Platform | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| **SMB/CIFS** | Windows, macOS | Native network drive mapping |
| **NFS** | Linux/Unix | Native mount via `/etc/fstab` |
| **WebDAV** | Any platform | Access via WebDAV-compatible clients |
| **WebDisk (Browser)** | Any platform | Browser-based file access with snapshot support |

#### Platform-Specific Access

| Platform | Recommended Protocol | Notes |
|----------|---------------------|-------|
| **Windows** | SMB | Map as network drive |
| **macOS** | SMB or NFS | Connect via Finder or mount |
| **Linux** | NFS | Mount in `/etc/fstab` |
| **Browser** | WebDisk | No client software needed |

### NAS Partial Administrators

Organizational units can designate **partial administrators** for NAS storage management. Partial administrators can:

- Manage access permissions for their unit's storage
- Set up directory structures
- Configure access for individual users and groups

Setup instructions for NAS partial administrators are available in the IT Service Center FAQ.

### Storage Specifications

| Specification | Value |
|--------------|-------|
| **Protocols** | NFS, SMB (CIFS), WebDAV |
| **Browser Access** | WebDisk |
| **Network Access** | MWN network + VPN |
| **Authentication** | TUM Central LDAP |
| **Backup** | Automatic via LRZ backup services |
| **Limitations** | File size limits and file name length restrictions apply |

### Access Control

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "NAS Access Control Flow"
flowchart LR
    User["User"]
    User --> |authenticates| LDAP["TUM Central LDAP"]
    LDAP --> |verifies| Groups["User Groups"]
    Groups --> |determines| Access["Access Permissions"]
    Access --> |grants/denies| NAS["NAS Shares"]
```

User and group management for NAS access:

- **Users**: Managed through TUM Central LDAP
- **Groups**: Created and managed via TUMonline
- **Permissions**: Set by administrators or partial administrators per share/directory
- **Windows permissions**: Can be configured directly on Windows clients

### Requesting Project Storage

To request project storage (Projektlaufwerk) for your organizational unit:

1. **Contact IT Service Center**: Email it-support@tum.de
2. **Define Requirements**:
   - Expected storage capacity
   - Number of users
   - Access patterns and permissions
   - Security requirements
3. **Provisioning**: LRZ sets up the storage
4. **Configuration**: Administrator configures user access

::: {.callout-tip}
## Consultation Available
The IT Service Center offers consultation for designing your storage setup. Contact it-support@tum.de for assistance.
:::

## Backup and File Recovery

### LRZ Backup

All NAS data is protected by LRZ backup services:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Backup Architecture"
flowchart TB
    subgraph Source["Source Data"]
        NAS["NAS Storage"]
    end

    subgraph Backup["LRZ Backup Infrastructure"]
        Agent["Backup Agent"]
        Server["Backup Server"]
        Storage["Backup Storage"]
    end

    NAS --> |automatic backup| Agent
    Agent --> Server
    Server --> Storage
```

### Backup Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Frequency** | Regular automatic backups |
| **Type** | Incremental (only changed files) |
| **Retention** | Multiple recovery points |
| **Verification** | Automatic integrity checks |
| **Storage** | Secure LRZ infrastructure |

### File Recovery Options

There are two ways to recover files:

#### 1. WebDisk Snapshots (Self-Service)

Users can recover accidentally deleted files themselves using WebDisk's snapshot feature:

- Access WebDisk via browser
- Navigate to the snapshot/version history
- Restore previous versions of files

::: {.callout-tip}
## Self-Service Recovery
Before contacting IT support for file restoration, check if you can recover the file yourself via WebDisk snapshots. This is faster and doesn't require a support ticket.
:::

#### 2. IT Service Center Restoration (Assisted)

For files not recoverable via WebDisk:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Data Restoration Process"
sequenceDiagram
    participant Admin as Administrator
    participant ITSZ as IT Service Center
    participant Backup as LRZ Backup

    Admin->>ITSZ: Restoration Request<br/>(via email)
    Note over Admin,ITSZ: Include: Path, Date, Description

    ITSZ->>Backup: Locate backup version
    Backup-->>ITSZ: Identify available snapshots
    ITSZ->>Backup: Initiate restoration
    Backup-->>ITSZ: Restoration complete
    ITSZ-->>Admin: Notification + location
```

#### Requesting Data Restoration

1. **Email**: it-support@tum.de
2. **Subject**: Data Restoration Request - [Department Name]
3. **Include**:
   - Full path to the file(s) or directory
   - Target date (which backup to restore from)
   - Description of what needs restoration
4. **Timing**: Request as soon as possible after data loss

::: {.callout-important}
## Act Quickly
Request restoration as soon as possible. While LRZ maintains multiple recovery points, earlier contact ensures best restoration options.
:::

### Required Information for Restoration

| Information | Description | Example |
|-------------|-------------|---------|
| **Path** | Full file/directory path | `/nas/department/projects/data.csv` |
| **Date** | Backup date to restore from | `2025-01-15` |
| **Type** | File or directory | Directory with all contents |
| **Urgency** | Priority level | Standard / Urgent |

## Storage Best Practices

### Data Organization

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Recommended Folder Structure"
flowchart TB
    Root["Department NAS Root"]
    Root --> Projects["Projects/"]
    Root --> Users["Users/"]
    Root --> Shared["Shared/"]
    Root --> Archive["Archive/"]

    Projects --> P1["Project_A/"]
    Projects --> P2["Project_B/"]

    Users --> U1["Mueller/"]
    Users --> U2["Schmidt/"]

    Shared --> Templates["Templates/"]
    Shared --> Resources["Resources/"]
```

### Recommendations

| Practice | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **Organized Structure** | Use logical folder hierarchy |
| **Clear Naming** | Use descriptive file and folder names (mind length limits) |
| **Regular Cleanup** | Archive or delete obsolete data |
| **Access Control** | Apply appropriate permissions |
| **Documentation** | Document folder purposes |
| **Use www-public** | For files that need web download access |

### What NOT to Store

- Temporary/cache files
- Personally identifiable data (without approval)
- Licensed software installers (use official sources)
- Extremely large media files (consider alternatives)

## Monitoring and Quotas

### Storage Monitoring

Administrators should regularly monitor:

- Current storage usage (check via WebDisk or mount point)
- Usage trends over time
- Large files and directories
- Inactive data candidates for archiving

### Quota Management

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Expansion** | Contact IT Service Center for additional needs |
| **Monitoring** | Regular usage reports available |
| **Alerts** | Notification before quota exceeded |

## Summary

| Service | Description | Contact |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| **Personal Storage** | Automatic for TUM members | Automatic |
| **Project Storage** | For organizational units | it-support@tum.de |
| **Access Management** | User/group permissions | TUMonline + partial admins |
| **Self-Service Recovery** | WebDisk snapshots | Self-service |
| **Assisted Recovery** | Full backup restoration | it-support@tum.de |
| **Quota Increase** | Additional storage | it-support@tum.de |
